title: Malus betulifolia
taxonomy: Malus betulifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It belongs to the genus Malus, which includes various species of apples and crabapples. The genus Malus is further classified under the subfamily Amygdaloideae. Malus betulifolia is native to China and is often used as an ornamental plant. Its classification as a species of flowering plant is consistent with the taxonomy of the Rosaceae family.
uses: Malus betulifolia, also known as the Chinese crabapple, has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which may help to treat various health conditions. In traditional medicine, the bark and leaves of Malus betulifolia are used to treat fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been studied for their potential antiviral and antioxidant properties. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Malus betulifolia's medicinal uses.

constituents: Malus betulifolia is a species of apple tree, and it is rich in various active constituents. These constituents include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
